rulings.Case Title: CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH VS. FOOD MARKET MANAGEMENT, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Center For Environmental Health Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Safeway Inc.S Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions (For Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, July 29, 2014, Line 1, Plaintiff Center For Environmental Health's Motion To Compel Defendant Safeway Inc.S Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions (For Tentative Ruling Only) (part 2 of 2 of tentative ruling) (continued part 2 of 2 of tentative ruling) Interrogatories #4.1, 4.2, 12.1, 12.2, 12.3, 12.4, 12.6, 14.2: All objections are overruled. Interrogatory 15.1: All objections are overruled. Defendant's statement of not applicable to subparts (b) and (c) is not a proper response. [See CCP 2030.210-240] Defendant to provide a further response specifically answering subparts (a), (b) and (c). If there are no facts, witnesses or documents then the response should so state. Interrogatory 17.1: All objections are overruled. Defendant to provide a further response. Defendant to serve a verified response to Form Interrogatories # 1.1, 3.7, 15.1, and 17.1 #1-25 no later than September 2, 2014. The court finds that defendant Safeway failed to meet its obligations under CCP 2030.210-240, CCP 2031.210-240 and CCP 2033.210-240 in responding to discovery. See Clement v. Alegre (2009) 177 CA4th 1277. The court further finds that defendant Safeway failed to meet and confer in good faith. See Townsend v. Superior Court (1998) 61 CA 4th 1431. Sanctions in the amount of $3560 for attorneys fees and the cost for filing the motion to be made payable to Center for Environmental Health and their attorneys of record, Lexington Law Group, no later than September 2, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.(end of 2 of 2 of tentative ruling) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH VS. FOOD MARKET MANAGEMENT, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Safeway Inc.S Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, July 29, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH's Motion To Compel Defendant Safeway Inc.S Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions (part 1 of 2 of tentative ruling) Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to Special Interrogatories, Requests for Production of Documents and Requests for Admissions is granted as follows: SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, Set #1 Defendant's General Objections are stricken. [See CCP 2030.210-240] Interrogatories #1 - 57: All objections are overruled. Defendant to serve a verified response to Interrogatories #1 -57 no later than September 2, 2014. REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, Set #1 Defendant's General Objections are stricken. [See CCP 2031.210-240] Defendant failed to provide a proper response as required by CCP 2031.240 for all documents that they are objecting to. Requests # 1 - 26: All objections are overruled. Defendant to serve a verified response and produce documents to Requests #1-26 no later than September 2, 2014. REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS, Set #1 Defendant's General Objections are stricken. [See CCP 2033.210-240] Requests # 1 - 26: All objections are overruled. Defendant to serve a verified response to Requests #1-25 no later than September 2, 2014. FORM INTERROGATORIES, Set #1 Defendant's General Objections are stricken. [See CCP 2030.210-240] Interrogatory #1.1: All objections are overruled. Defendant to provide a further responses that states the name, address, telephone number and relationship of the to you of each PERSON who prepared or assisted in the preparation of the responses to Form Interrogatories, Set #1. Interrogatories #3.1, 3.2, 3.3, 3.4, 3.5, 3.6, : All objections are overruled. Interrogatory #3.7: All objections are overruled. Defendant to provide a further response specifically answering subparts (a), (b) and (c). (end of part 1 of 2)=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA JOYA VS. LLOYD GONZALES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Neuropsychological Examination Of Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, July 29, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANT HERTZ VEHICLES,LLC, THE HERTZ CORPORATION's Motion To Compel Neuropsychological Examination Of Plaintiff Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion to compel Neuropsychological Examination of Plaintiff is granted as follows: 1. Dr. McNeil may conduct the examination over two days, Friday, August 1 and Monday, August 4. The mental examinations will start at 9:00 a.m. Each day of examination will be limited to 8 hours with a total time of 16 hours. 2. The examination may be recorded. 3. The standardized tests are limited to those listed in paragraph 5 Dr. McNeil's declaration. 4.Dr. McNeil may not the following questions in his examination of plaintiff: (a)Why plaintiff filed suit and what she hopes to gain from the lawsuit including compensation (b)Why plaintiff hired an attorney and any attorney/client communications (c)Any legal contention or why plaintiff believes defendants or anyone else are at fault for her injuries (d)Plaintiff's finances including sources of income other than the wage loss allegedly caused by the accident (e)Specific questions about speeds, forces, distances or times that are to be used for accident reconstruction (f)The names and/or identities of sexual partners (g)Other injuries not related to the same areas of the body that is the subject of the lawsuit. Plaintiff may avoid the mental examination by offering to stipulate that mo claim is being made for mental and emotional distress "over and above that usually associated with the physical injuries claimed" and no psychiatric testimony will be offered at trial in support of any claimed emotional distress. [See CCP 2032.230(c)] Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ADELAIDA GONGORA VS. MOHAMMAD, NADEEM, D/B/A CITY PIZZA AND YUCATECAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Order That Matters In Req For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, July 29, 2014, Line 4,PLAINTIFF ADELAIDA GONGORA's Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Opposition filed two days late, but was considered. Defendants' opposition fails to show that verified responses were in fact served on the plaintiff as the exhibits only provide the first page of the responses and the proof of serve. Defendants failed to show the court that their responses to requests for admissions were in "substantial compliance" with CCP 2033.210-230. The court also does not consider Defense counsel's declaration persuasive. The trial date in this matter is August 18, 2014. Defendants and their counsel were aware of the trial date since they were served by the court on January 8, 2014 with the trial date notice. To date there has been neither a motion filed for continuance of trial nor a court order granting a continuance of trial. Furthermore, the discovery was served on May 2, 2014-almost three months ago. According to defense counsel's declaration, he did not learn that Nadeem Mohammad was out of the country until three weeks after the verified responses were due. Plaintiff's motion for Matters in Requests for Admissions be deemed admitted is Granted. Plaintiff's request for sanctions against Defendants Nadeem Mohammad, Shabbir Mughal and their attorney of Record, Matthew J. Webb is granted pursuant to CCP 2033.280(c). Sanctions in the amount of $1,012.50 is to be made payable to Adelaida Gongora and her attorneys of record, Liberation Law Group no later than August 18, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, July 29, 2014, Line 4, PLAINTIFF ADELAIDA GONGORA's Motion To Compel Responses In Support Of Monetary Sanction Request (for tentative ruling only) (part 1 of 2) Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Opposition filed two days late, but was considered. Defendants' opposition fails to show that verified responses were in fact served on the plaintiff as the exhibits only provide the first page of the responses and the proof of serve. The court also does not consider Defense counsel's declaration persuasive. The trial date in this matter is August 18, 2014. Defendants and their counsel were aware of the trial date since they were served by the court on January 8, 2014 with the trial date notice. To date there has been neither a motion filed for continuance of trial nor a court order granting a continuance of trial. Furthermore, the discovery was served on May 2, 2014-almost three months ago. According to defense counsel's declaration, he did not learn that Nadeem Mohammad was out of the country until three weeks after the verified responses were due. Plaintiff's motion to compel responses to: (1)Form Interrogatories, Set #1-General; (2) Form Interrogatories-Employment Law, Set #1, Special Interrogatories Set, #1; and Request for Production of Documents, Set #1 on Nadeem Mohammad is granted. Defendant Nadeem Mohammad to serve verified responses and produce documents no later than Monday, August 4, 2014. Service to be effectuated by hand delivery or overnight service. Plaintiff's motion to compel responses to: (1)Form Interrogatories, Set #1-General; (2) Form Interrogatories-Employment Law, Set #1, Special Interrogatories Set, #1; and Request for Production of Documents, Set #1 on Shabbir Mughal is granted. Defendant Nadeem Mohammad to serve verified responses and produce documents no later than Monday, August 4, 2014. Service to be effectuated by hand delivery or overnight service. Plaintiff's request for sanctions against Defendants Nadeem Mohammad, Shabbir Mughal and their attorney of Record, Matthew J. Webb is granted. Sanctions in the amount of $2348.75 is to be made payable to Adelaida Gongora and her attorneys of record, Liberation Law Group no later than August 18, 2014. (end of part 1 of 2)=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN GIDDING VS. GLENDONBROOK WINES PTY LTD, A PRESUMED AUSTRALIAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 29, 2014, Line 9: DEFENDANT ROBERT WHITE'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The motion is granted. Defendant establishes that this is a SLAPP suit. Plaintiff's claims are based on defendant's legal representation of Glendonbrook Wines. "All communicative acts performed by attorneys as part of their representation of a client in a judicial proceeding or other petitioning context are per se protected as petitioning activity by the anti-SLAPP statute." Cabral v. Martins (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 471, 480. Therefore, the burden has shifted to plaintiff to present evidence showing a probability of prevailing on the merits. Plaintiff has failed to maintain his burden. First, he does not present any evidence, but merely relies on his unverified complaint. Second, Civil Cod 47 [litigation privilege] bars plaintiff's claims. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: DIANA MADULI VS. JASON GOBBELL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 29, 2014, Line 11: DEFENDANT ROSITA GOBBELL'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Continued to August 5, 2014 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than July 29, 2014 at 4:00 p.m. or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)
